what_it_does: This file is a model-generated interpretive ledger entry for the TypeBuilder.jsx React canvas. It stores a structured summary of the component's behavior, internal state helpers, Vi-assisted authoring flows, save/fork/delete semantics, layout and editing modes, conventions, concerns, questions, themes, novelty, contribution, and embedding-ready summaries, and it explicitly declares that it contains no code symbols.
observations: The file is a single JSON object following a fixed interpretive schema with fields like what_it_does, observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, notes, questions, purpose_vs_actual, apparent_themes, intention_signals, novelty, contribution, summary_for_embedding, and intention_for_embedding.
It was produced by the model 'opus' and annotates the source path 'app/canvases/TypeBuilder.jsx' within project 'claude-ds'.

It describes TypeBuilder as a universal Type authoring canvas that holds a mutable draft Type, provides patch helpers for slots and variables, supports three layouts (split/wizard/canvas) and four editing modes (visual/form/vi/json).
It records Vi-assisted moves: viPropose generates a Type from a brief, viSuggestSlots requires the Type to be saved first, and failures route to window.dsaToast.

It documents registry-as-truth behavior: all reads/writes go through CV_REGISTRY, parent candidates are filtered to the same layer, and editing a built-in Type forks it into user space with provenance 'user' and forkedFrom.

It notes that inner panels such as BuilderLibrary, Preview, and Inspector live in TypeBuilder2.jsx, and it asks questions about the API surface of window.CV_TYPES_VI and the location of several sub-components.
It ends with symbols[] = [], indicating no code symbols were extracted from this interpretive file.
standouts: It is an interpretive meta-layer rather than source code, capturing design rationale and usage semantics in a queryable JSON form.
It highlights the built-in fork-on-edit lineage rule and the same-layer extends constraint as first-class design decisions.
It maps three layout shells and four editing modes onto a single draft model, framing TypeBuilder as a universal authoring surface.
It explicitly declares an empty symbol set, making the boundary between interpretation and code structure clear.
conventions: Follows the ledger interpretive schema by separating factual description, notable observations, conventions, concerns, questions, themes, novelty, and embedding summaries.
Uses a neutral, descriptive tone and avoids ranking language.
Preserves the original component's metadata (project, path, model) inside the annotation object.
Cites window-global patterns, registry-as-truth, immutable state updates, and provenance/forkedFrom conventions from the described component.
References cross-file dependencies (TypeBuilder2.jsx, CV_TYPES_VI) as open questions rather than asserted facts.
concerns: The file makes many detailed implementation claims that cannot be verified from this JSON alone; they depend on the accuracy of the underlying source and the prior model.
The symbols array is empty, so structural relationships such as imports, calls, and internal function definitions are not captured here.

It mixes inherited implementation concerns (missing global, native confirm dialogs, split file dependency, id collision) with the interpretive record rather than isolating them as source-derived assertions.
The 'project' field value 'claude-ds' differs from the ledger path's project 'company', which could confuse indexing if not normalized.
notes: A structured interpretive snapshot of the TypeBuilder.jsx canvas, summarizing its universal Type authoring surface, draft model, Vi helpers, and registry save/fork semantics for a fused ledger.
